content.text: I found the serializer logic: `NodeSerializer.serialize` treats any value with an `@idRef` member trait as a shape ID and always writes it via the `$I` formatter, which can add a `use` and render a relative unquoted syntactic shape ID. Next I’m checking the `IdRefTrait` API/tests to confirm the intended distinction for missing targets before adding the failing test.

Implemented the fix in `smithy-model/src/main/java/software/amazon/smithy/model/shapes/SmithyIdlModelSerializer.java`.

Summary:
- Found the issue in `NodeSerializer.serializeShapeId`.
- Previously, all `@idRef` string values were emitted through `$I`, causing missing shape IDs to be rendered as syntactic shape IDs and imported with `use`.
- Updated serialization so `@idRef` values are emitted as shape IDs only when the referenced shape exists in the model; otherwise they remain quoted strings.

Validation:
- Added a temporary focused regression test reproducing the reported issue.
- Confirmed the test passed with:
  - `./gradlew :smithy-model:test --tests software.amazon.smithy.model.shapes.SmithyIdlModelSerializerTest.quotesIdRefsToMissingShapes`
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Confirmed remaining code compiles with:
  - `./gradlew :smithy-model:compileJava`

Current working tree:
- Only `SmithyIdlModelSerializer.java` is modified.
- No commits were made.
